necessityProof.blockerResolved: Before real shard or cross-project execution, operators need a local read-only way to see whether Node has touched Java or mini-kv, whether failures are errors or timeouts, and whether latency is changing.
necessityProof.laterConsumer: Future shard preview and real-read rehearsals can consume /api/v1/metrics as a diagnostic source without changing Java or mini-kv contracts.
necessityProof.reuseDecision: Existing readiness and audit reports describe static gates and request audit events, but they do not aggregate upstream client attempts or latency percentiles.
necessityProof.growthStopCondition: N4 stops at one shared registry and one route; new upstream clients should register in the same service instead of creating per-route observability reports.

deliverables: src/app.ts request-id correlation and inline metrics registration
代码讲解记录_生产雏形阶段3/r2000/2089-production-excellence-node-observability-v2118.md

runtimeContract.safeDefault: With UPSTREAM_PROBES_ENABLED=false and UPSTREAM_ACTIONS_ENABLED=false, the endpoint reports zero upstream attempts and does not touch Java or mini-kv.

focusedN4Batch.command: npx vitest run test/upstreamMetrics.test.ts test/metricsRoutes.test.ts test/accessPolicyProfile.test.ts test/accessGuard.test.ts -- --testTimeout=180000

focusedRatchetAndN4Batch.command: npx vitest run test/governanceGrowthRatchet.test.ts test/upstreamMetrics.test.ts test/metricsRoutes.test.ts test/accessPolicyProfile.test.ts test/accessGuard.test.ts -- --testTimeout=180000

localHttpSmoke.command: node dist/server.js on 127.0.0.1:4191, then GET /health, /api/v1/metrics, and release readiness gate

coverage.ratchetCorrection: The first N4 coverage run failed because the initial cut added one src/services file and one src/routes file. The registry was moved under src/clients and metrics route registration was folded into src/app.ts before the passing rerun.

backgroundProcessesStarted: node dist/server.js for local smoke, stopped in script finally

crossProjectParallel.reason: N4 observes Node's own client attempts and does not require fresh Java or mini-kv evidence.
